channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump admin makes new move to bring South African refugees to US as president blasts nation's rulers again
item.description: As the U.S. begins refugee processing for South African Afrikaners, a group is pushing Washington to support its bid for an autonomous Afrikaner homeland within South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian missiles hit Ukrainian city of Sumy during Palm Sunday celebrations, killing more than 30
item.description: Russian missiles struck the heart of the Ukrainian city of Sumy as people gathered to celebrate Palm Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Voters in Gabon await results of presidential election, with likely victory for coup leader
item.description: LIBREVILLE, Gabon (AP) — Voters in Gabon on Sunday awaited the results of the central African nation’s presidential election, which the country’s military rulers hope will legitimize their grip on power after they staged a coup in 2023. The interim president, Gen.
item.dc:creator: YVES LAURENT GOMA and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southeast Asia water festivals begin, but earthquake recovery blunts Myanmar's celebrations
item.description: Several Southeast Asian countries are kicking off their annual water festival holiday, an occasion for merrymaking during what is usually the hottest time of the year.
item.dc:creator: Grant Peck, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New gear could keep California crab fishermen on the water longer, and whales safe
item.description: After years of a shortened crab fishing season aimed at preventing whale entanglements off the West Coast, California crabbers are experimenting with a new fishing method that allows them to stay on the water longer while keeping the marine mammals safe.
item.dc:creator: Amy Taxin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of failing to pause strikes after US envoy leaves Moscow
item.description: Russia and Ukraine’s top diplomats on Saturday used a high-level conference in Turkey to once again trade accusations of violating a tentative U.S.-brokered deal to pause strikes on energy infrastructure, underscoring the challenges of negotiating an end to the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump team holds 'constructive' face-to-face nuclear talks with Iran, will meet again next weekend
item.description: The U.S. and Iran held rare face-to-face nuclear talks in Oman Saturday. The Trump administration is pushing for diplomacy backed by military pressure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dominican club collapse death toll rises to 226 after woman pulled from rubble dies in hospital
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — A woman hospitalized after being rescued from the rubble of a roof collapse at a popular nightclub in the Dominican Republic died on Saturday, raising the death toll to 226, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Serbia's populist president demands order after months of anti-corruption protests shaking his rule
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic on Saturday demanded that authorities restore “order and peace” in the Balkan country following months of anti-corruption protests that have shaken his firm grip on power.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: High winds and sandstorms prompt Beijing to cancel hundreds of flights and close parks
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — High winds and the threat of sandstorms Saturday prompted Beijing to cancel hundreds of flights and close public parks, as massive gales felled hundreds of trees, crushing cars and damaging older homes across China’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico to make ‘immediate’ water payment to US as Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Hours after President Donald Trump threatened Mexico with additional tariffs over a massive water debt, that country's president publicly vowed to make a substantial payment soon.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Mideast mediator Oman at the center of a key first Iran-US meeting over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: MUSCAT, Oman (AP) — Once again, some of the highest stakes in Middle East geopolitics will be discussed in this quiet coastal city without skyscrapers.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS ends protections from deportation for Afghanistan, Cameroon
item.description: Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Kristi Noem has stripped protections from deportation for Afghans and Cameroonians in the U.S., calling into question the ability of some Afghan evacuees to remain in America.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In a turbulent time, Turkey lays claim to a role of stability for Europe, Syria and global trade
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan on Friday sought to position his country as a point of stability in an increasingly tumultuous world, saying Turkey was essential for European security and able to span geopolitical divisions over Ukraine, Syria and U.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Crowds demonstrate against gender-based violence in South Africa after alleged rape of 7-year-old
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa. (AP) — A crowd of protesters staged a demonstration against gender-based violence in the South African capital of Pretoria on Friday as part of a national outcry after an alleged rape of a 7-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The UN decries ongoing combat in Myanmar as earthquake relief faces big challenges
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Human rights experts for the United Nations are expressing urgent concern about ongoing military operations in Myanmar’s civil war, despite ceasefires called by major parties to facilitate relief efforts after the country’s devastating March 28 earthquake. At the same time, a new U.N.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin ordered to return alleged MS-13 member mistakenly deported
item.description: The justices did not say exactly what Kilmar Abrego Garcia's release would entail or how he would return to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Greenpeace UK leader arrested for targeting American embassy with ' blood-red dye’: report
item.description: Greenpeace’s U.K. leader and five other activists reportedly were arrested after tossing hundreds of liters of “blood-red dye" into a pond at the U.S. embassy in London.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwan's president targets China influence, kicks out pro-Beijing agitators amid rising tensions
item.description: Taiwanese President William Lai promises an uncompressing stance against both domestic and foreign pro-CCP influence operations amid rising tensions between Taipei and Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: US, Panama ‘taking back’ canal from ‘China's influence,’ says Hegseth
item.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth said the U.S. and Panama are strengthening security ties and "together" will "take back" the canal from the influence of China, which operates two major ports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas, with video showing him getting nipped by the bird while his son laughed in his lap.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: US admiral at NATO fired in expanding national security purge
item.description: U.S. Navy Vice Admiral Shoshana Chatfield is one of only a handful of female Navy three-star officers.
item.dc:creator: Phil Stewart, Idrees Ali, Lili Bayer / Reuters, NewsNation
